_purpose: Self-descriptions of each tool's UNMAPPED rules, sourced VERBATIM/faithfully from that tool's OWN source (cited), for the symmetric Codex semantic pass. Each tool describes its own rule; the architect does not characterize either tool's rules. Lead-auditable against the cited source.

modal-as-first-thought.description: Modals used where inline editing or progressive disclosure would work.

taste/border-radius-inconsistency.description: Many distinct border-radius literals are used instead of 1-2 named tokens from a radius scale.

taste/hero-radial-blob.description: A radial-gradient is used as the background inside a hero/banner selector - the canonical AI-slop hero backdrop in place of an editorial image or typography-first hero.

taste/large-inline-style.description: A large inline <style> block sits in the document head instead of living in the external stylesheet.

taste/translatey-in-hover.description: A hover state lifts an element with transform: translateY instead of the prescribed scale-on-press interaction.

side-tab.description: Thick colored border on one side of a card - the most recognizable tell of AI-generated UIs.

border-accent-on-rounded.description: Thick accent border on a rounded card - the border clashes with the rounded corners.

overused-font.description: Overused default faces (Inter, Roboto, Fraunces, Geist, Plus Jakarta Sans, Space Grotesk) used on so many sites they no longer feel distinctive.

flat-type-hierarchy.description: Font sizes are too close together, giving no clear visual type hierarchy.

monotonous-spacing.description: The same spacing value used everywhere, with no rhythm or variation.

em-dash-overuse.description: More than two em-dashes in body copy, an AI cadence tell.
